hypothesis: Enhancing aromatic complementarity at position 5 and introducing steric/charge features at position 6 will improve SSTR2 selectivity by stabilizing SSTR2-specific interactions while reducing off-target affinity.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis is that disrupting off-target interactions at non-pharmacophore positions will increase SSTR2 selectivity. The next iteration should focus on these positions to improve Δmargin beyond the native SST-14.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are non-pharmacophore and are likely involved in off-target interactions (especially with SSTR3 and SSTR5). Focusing design on these positions can help disrupt off-target binding while preserving key pharmacophore residues.
These mutations are designed to reduce conserved pocket interactions with off-target SSTRs while maintaining overall binding affinity and structural integrity.
